zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Bourne於1322年出生在英國諾福爾可易亞斯特 哈林時，她的父親Nicholas Bourn32歲，母親Mrs. Nicholas Bourn32歲。她於大約1345年與Sir Thomas de Gyney or Jenney在英國英格蘭素福爾可結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1390年在英國素福爾可可諾迪沙拉爾 煙雨 布可洛福享年68歲。

nameMeanings.name: Bourne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived beside a stream, from southern Middle English bourne, Old English burna, burne ‘spring, stream’, or a habitational name from a place called with this word, for example Bourn in Cambridgeshire or Bourne in Lincolnshire. In surnames the reference is often to an old stream called burna, surviving as the name of a farm. This word was replaced as the general word for a stream in southern dialects by Old English brōc (see Brook ) and came to be restricted in meaning to a stream flowing only intermittently, especially in winter.

variants.name: Burn
meanings: English (Northumberland and Durham): variant of   Bourne  .

variants.name: Born
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Heinz, Helmut, Berthold, Frieda, Friedrich, Fritz, Gerhard, Horst, Jutta.
English (of Norman origin): nickname from Old French borgne ‘one-eyed, squint-eyed’.

variants.name: Cockburn
meanings: Scottish (Midlothian) and northern English: habitational name from a place in Berwickshire named Cockburn, from Old English cocc ‘cock, rooster’ (or the related byname Cocca) + burna ‘stream’ (see Bourne ). This surname is traditionally pronounced Coburn.

variants.name: Bournes
meanings: English: variant of Bourne with plural or post-medieval exrescent -s.

variants.name: Brook
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived by a brook, stream, water-meadows or low, marshy ground, from Middle English brook, Old English brōc ‘brook, stream’; or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, such as Brook (Kent, Rutland) or Brooke (Norfolk).
North German: topographic name for someone who lived by a water meadow or marsh, from Low German brook (cognate with German Bruch ‘swamp’ and Old English brōc; see 1 above).
Americanized form of Dutch Broek: topographic name from broek, a cognate of 2 above.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Bourne was born in 1322, in East Harling, Norfolk, England, her father, Nicholas Bourn, was 32 and her mother, Mrs. Nicholas Bourn, was 32. She married Sir Thomas de Gyney or Jenney about 1345, in Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died in 1390, in Knodishall with Buxlow, Suffolk, England, at the age of 68.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Bourne est née en 1322, à East Harling, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Nicholas Bourn, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Nicholas Bourn, avait 32 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Thomas de Gyney or Jenney environ 1345, à Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1390, à Knodishall with Buxlow, Suffolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 68 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Bourneが1322年にイギリスノーフォルクエアスト ハーリングで生まれた時，父Nicholas Bournは32歳，母Mrs. Nicholas Bournは32歳であった。約1345年にイギリスイギリススフォルクでSir Thomas de Gyney or Jenneyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1390年にイギリススフォルクノディシャル ウィス ブクスロウで68歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Bourne(이)가 1322년 영국 노르폴크 이스트 하를링에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Nicholas Bourn(은)는 32세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mrs. Nicholas Bourn(은)는 32세였다. 그녀는 대략 1345년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크에서 Sir Thomas de Gyney or Jenney(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1390년 영국 수폴크 노디샤 위스 버스로에서 68세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ela casou-se com Sir Thomas de Gyney or Jenney aproximadamente 1345, em Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Bourne родилась в 1322 г. в местности эаст харлинг, норфолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Nicholas Bourn было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Nicholas Bourn, 32. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Thomas de Gyney or Jenney около 1345 г. в местности суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1390 г. в местности нодишолл вит букслоу, суффолк, Англия в возрасте 68 лет.
